systemicPatterns.pattern: ConceptTag mismatches - scenarios teaching different concepts than their tags indicate
Score perspective errors - home/away confusion in Top vs Bottom innings
Repetitive explanations that don't teach different principles
Best answer designation doesn't match rate distributions
Invalid position categories - 'rules' and 'famous' used instead of standard positions
Factual errors about force plays and game mechanics
Age-inappropriate complexity - advanced stats/concepts for young players

blindSpots.description: Current criteria don't check if explanations actually teach the conceptTag principle
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Verify each explanation explicitly connects to the tagged concept

blindSpots.description: No validation that home/away team perspective matches inning half
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Cross-check score display with Top/Bottom inning designation

blindSpots.description: Missing check for whether best answer's rate is highest among options
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Validate that best=X has the highest rate value

blindSpots.description: No verification that position categories match standard baseball positions
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Validate position against approved list: pitcher, catcher, firstBase, etc.

blindSpots.description: Explanations not checked for teaching variety - can be repetitive
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Ensure each explanation teaches a different aspect or principle

blindSpots.description: Youth baseball realism not validated - unrealistic pitch arsenals for age
blindSpots.proposedCheck: Verify pitch types and strategic complexity match the difficulty/age rating

newRules.name: ConceptTag Alignment
newRules.description: Scenario content must directly teach the principle indicated by conceptTag

newRules.name: Score Perspective Check
newRules.description: In Top innings, away team bats and home team fields; score display must match this perspective

newRules.name: Best Answer Rate Validation
newRules.description: The option marked as 'best' must have the highest rate value among all options

newRules.name: Valid Position Categories
newRules.description: Position must be from: pitcher, catcher, firstBase, secondBase, thirdBase, shortstop, leftField, centerField, rightField, batter, baserunner, manager

newRules.name: Explanation Teaching Variety
newRules.description: Each explanation must teach a different principle or aspect - no repetitive messaging

newRules.name: Force Play Accuracy
newRules.description: Force play mechanics must be correct - stepping on a base removes force at subsequent bases

newRules.name: Age-Appropriate Complexity
newRules.description: Pitch types, statistics, and strategic concepts must match difficulty level (diff:1 = basic, diff:3 = advanced)

newRules.name: Explanation Index Alignment
newRules.description: Each explanation must address the option matching its index (explanation[0] for option[0])

newRules.name: Relay vs Cutoff Distinction
newRules.description: Use 'relay' for deep outfield throws requiring middle infielder, 'cutoff' for shorter throws that might be cut

newRules.name: Count Display Requirement
newRules.description: Count should show specific balls-strikes (e.g., '2-1'), not placeholder '-'

pitcher: Verify pitch selection matches age/difficulty level
Check pickoff scenarios actually involve pickoff mechanics
Ensure pitch location and movement descriptions are realistic
catcher: Validate blocking technique descriptions match proper form
Check pitch calling logic uses realistic game context
Verify throw-down scenarios include accurate timing
firstBase: Ensure stretch technique is properly described
Validate force/tag play understanding at first base
secondBase: Verify double play pivot footwork is correct
thirdBase: Validate hot corner reaction time scenarios
shortstop: Verify hole coverage on steal attempts
centerField: Verify priority caller status is maintained
batter: Ensure count leverage is properly taught
baserunner: Validate steal timing and breakeven rates
Ensure base coach communication is included
manager: Verify strategic decisions have clear rationale
Ensure decisions match game situation pressure

enhancedPromptAdditions:
ADDITIONAL VALIDATION REQUIREMENTS:

1. CONCEPTTAG ALIGNMENT: Reject if scenario doesn't directly teach the concept in conceptTag
2. SCORE PERSPECTIVE: Warn if Top inning shows home team ahead (should be behind)
3. BEST ANSWER RATES: Reject if best answer doesn't have highest rate value
4. VALID POSITIONS ONLY: pitcher|catcher|firstBase|secondBase|thirdBase|shortstop|leftField|centerField|rightField|batter|baserunner|manager
5. EXPLANATION VARIETY: Each explanation must teach different principle - no repetition
6. FORCE PLAY ACCURACY: Stepping on base removes subsequent forces
7. AGE-APPROPRIATE: Match complexity to difficulty (1=basic, 2=intermediate, 3=advanced)
8. EXPLANATION ALIGNMENT: explanation[i] must address option[i]
9. RELAY VS CUTOFF: Use correct terminology based on throw distance
10. COUNT DISPLAY: Show specific count (2-1) not placeholder (-)
